commit.message:
fix(billing): per-invoice download + Stripe customer reuse + credit-sync wallet sum (ECA-669) (#1616)

## Summary

Closes both halves of
[ECA-669](https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-669):

### Half 1 — invoice download "点 24$ 显示 100$"

Two compounding defects:

1. **Stripe customer not reused across checkouts.**
`web/src/app/api/stripe/create-checkout-session/route.ts` never passed
`customer=` to `stripe.checkout.sessions.create`, so Stripe spawned a
fresh customer object on every paid checkout. The
`checkout.session.completed` webhook then overwrote
`user.stripe_customer_id` with the newest one, orphaning the previous
customer (and all its invoices).
2. **Download button had no per-invoice scope.** Each row in
`InvoiceHistory.tsx` called the same no-arg `openCustomerPortal()`,
which mounts Stripe Billing Portal against `account.stripe_customer_id`.
Historical orphan invoices were unreachable regardless of which row was
clicked.

Prod audit: 71 paid Stripe orders missing `stripe_invoice_id` (63 sub +
8 topup, all `ORD-*`); 17 of those are orphan across 9 users (~$749).
Invariant validated: every paid order missing invoice_id has
`stripe_checkout_session_id`, enabling a single canonical resolution
path.

### Half 2 — credit-sync "订阅后左右积分不一致"

Reported on uid `7339182334568046592` (Antom Starter): left sidebar
shows `积分 48,346` while right `/claw-settings` 用量 panel shows `积分 33,120
/ 33,120` at the same time. Root cause: this user has **two active
subscription wallets in BG** (legacy duplicate from BG init).
`check_user_credits` iterated `wallets` and **assigned** balances per
match, so the duplicate overwrote the previous result and
`subscription_credits + topup_credits` no longer agreed with BG's own
`available_credits` (which had already summed everything).

Fix: sum across non-terminated wallets of each kind. Defensive — keeps
the display consistent regardless of how many duplicate wallets BG
carries.

## Approach

Design spec:
[`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-eca-669-invoice-download.md`](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-eca-669-invoice-download.md)
— sections 1–5 cover Half 1; section 6 covers Half 2.

### Half 1 — single canonical lookup path
- `resolve_invoice_id_from_session` in
`app/services/stripe/invoice_lookup.py` is the only definition. Applied
two places (no fallback chain):
- Webhook `_apply_base_order_update` writes `stripe_invoice_id` for
every new paid order.
- One-shot migration script (run out-of-band — see below) backfills the
71 historical rows.
- **Per-order download endpoint** `GET /orders/{order_id}/invoice`
resolves the order, retrieves the invoice, and 302s to Stripe's hosted
URL. Ownership is uid-only; orphan customer mismatches are intentionally
invisible at this layer.
- **Frontend Download** becomes a plain `<a target="_blank">` pointing
at the Next.js relay (`GET /api/orders/[orderId]/invoice`), which
propagates the 302 verbatim with the new `redirect: 'manual'` knob on
`proxyToBackend`.
- **Bug 1 fix** Next.js checkout route fetches `user.stripe_customer_id`
from the backend before calling Stripe and passes `customer=` when
present (omitting `customer_email`).

### Half 2 — sum wallet balances
- One-line shape change in `check_user_credits` (`=` → `+=`) keeps
`subscription_credits` / `topup_credits` consistent with BG's
`available_credits` regardless of duplicate wallets.

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **Migration script for the 71 historical orders is NOT in this PR.**
After merge + deploy, it's run via `kubectl exec` heredoc against the
production claw-interface pod (so the in-pod runtime carries the new
`resolve_invoice_id_from_session` helper). Plan is `--dry-run` first,
review output, then `--apply`.
- **BG duplicate-wallet cleanup** — uid `7339182334568046592` has 2
active subscription wallets in BG (one empty, one with the new balance).
Normally a user has only 1 sub + 1 topup. Cleaning up the duplicate BG
state is a separate operations task; the PR's defensive sum keeps the UI
consistent in the meantime.
- Merging the two Stripe customer objects in Stripe itself — Stripe
doesn't expose `customers.merge`. Orphan customers stay as-is; invoices
reachable through the new app-side endpoint.

## Test plan

- [x] `app/services/stripe/invoice_lookup.py` — 11 unit tests cover both
resolution modes, expanded vs id-only shapes, and the four error paths
in `fetch_hosted_invoice_url_for_order`.
- [x] `_apply_base_order_update` — 3 new tests cover happy path,
resolver returning `None`, and resolver raising (order must still be
marked paid).
- [x] `GET /orders/{order_id}/invoice` — wiring test confirms 302 with
the resolved URL.
- [x] `web/src/app/api/orders/[orderId]/invoice/route.ts` — 6 tests
cover happy redirect, missing Location header, non-redirect body relay,
and proxy failure.
- [x] `InvoiceHistory.tsx` — Download is an `<a href>` for paid Stripe
orders, hidden for Antom.
- [x] `create-checkout-session/route.ts` — 3 new tests verify `customer`
is passed when user has one, `customer_email` fallback when not, and
graceful degradation when backend lookup fails.
- [x]
`test_user_credits.py::test_duplicate_active_wallets_sum_not_overwrite`
— duplicate active sub wallets sum, terminated wallet skipped, and `sub
+ topup == available_credits` invariant.
- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm vitest run` all green.
- [x] `ruff check`, `pyright`, `pytest` green on touched files.
- [ ] **Post-deploy**: run `backfill_stripe_invoice_ids.py --dry-run`
against prod, review output, then `--apply`.
- [ ] **Post-deploy smoke**: as uid `7269557881928253440`, click
Download on the $24 row → confirm hosted invoice opens for the $24
invoice. As uid `7339182334568046592`, confirm left sidebar credits
matches right panel credits.

commit.message:
refactor(arch-review): extract inline prompt to .claude/commands/arch-review.md (#1617)

## Summary

The `Architectural Code Review` workflow embedded ~170 lines of LLM
prompt inside a yaml `with: prompt:` field. Extract those instructions
to a standard Claude Code slash command and have the workflow invoke
`/arch-review` with positional args.

**Why now:**
- The yaml mixed trigger/IO config with review methodology — diff &
review friction.
- No local entry point for a dry-run; iterating on the prompt required
dispatching a real GHA run that touches three production issues.
- Sibling `.claude/commands/feature.md` already validated the standard
frontmatter pattern; we just sat one level beneath that capability.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.claude/commands/arch-review.md` | **New.** Frontmatter
(`description` + `argument-hint`) + body (the review methodology, with
`$1`–`$5` positional args replacing GHA expressions). |
| `.github/workflows/claude-arch-review.yaml` | `prompt:` collapsed from
~170 lines inline to single line `/arch-review "$ARG1" "$ARG2" "$ARG3"
"$ARG4" "$ARG5"`. |

**Net diff**: -169 / +186 across two files. yaml -169, command +178
(frontmatter + inputs preamble + body).

## What stays the same

- `inputs.language` / `inputs.modules` (introduced in #1608) — unchanged
- `Resolve output language` step / `Snapshot prior baseline` step —
unchanged
- Python `Compose new issue body` step (incl. line-anchored
`manual_suffix` regex from #1614) — unchanged
- `Update issue body and title` / `Minimize stale review comments` —
unchanged
- ARCH_REVIEW marker schema / F-ID monotonic allocation / 8-week
resolved trim / verbatim still-present rule — unchanged

## Argument mapping

| `$N` | Replaces | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `$1` | `${{ matrix.module_name }}` | `Web Frontend` |
| `$2` | `${{ matrix.module_path }}` | `web/src` |
| `$3` | `${{ steps.lang.outputs.name }}` | `Simplified Chinese (简体中文)`
|
| `$4` | `${{ steps.prior.outputs.date }}` | `2026-05-13` |
| `$5` | `${{ steps.prior.outputs.next_id }}` | `12` |

File paths (`$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/prior_body.md`,
`$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/findings.md`) stay as env-var references in the
command body. CI runner provides `$GITHUB_WORKSPACE`; local dry-runs
need to export it manually.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`python3 -c "import yaml;
yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-arch-review.yaml'))"`)
- [x] SDK auto-discovers the new command: \`/arch-review\` shows up in
the in-session skill/command list with the correct description
- [ ] Local dry-run after merge:
- \`mkdir -p /tmp/arch-review-local && touch
/tmp/arch-review-local/prior_body.md\`
  - \`export GITHUB_WORKSPACE=/tmp/arch-review-local\`
- \`/arch-review "Web Frontend" web/src "Simplified Chinese (简体中文)"
2026-05-13 1\`
- Inspect \`findings.md\` — full ARCH_REVIEW marker set + Chinese
summary/finding text + English schema tokens
- [ ] GHA \`workflow_dispatch\` on iOS module (\`-f
modules=arch-review:ios -f language=zh\`): run succeeds, issue #365
stays at 1 line-anchored RESOLVED_END, new findings Chinese / schema
tokens English
- [ ] English regression: dispatch on claw-interface (\`-f
language=en\`) confirms the \`en\` path didn't drift

## Risks

- **Risk**: `claude-code-action@v1` fails to discover the new command
and treats the prompt as plain text. **Mitigation**: SDK already
auto-discovered the command locally during development (it appeared in
the in-session command list immediately on file creation). The action
runs the same SDK in CI on the checked-out repo.
- **Risk**: 5 positional args misordered when invoked from yaml.
**Mitigation**: Command body \`## Inputs\` section explicitly documents
each \`\$N\`. GHA \`prompt:\` uses the same order in a single line for
visual verification.
- **Roll-back**: Single commit revert; no data state to clean up.

commit.message:
refactor(web): RQ v2 PR-b3 — useBillingCredits switches to useQuery (#1537)

## Summary

- Migrate `useBillingCredits` from a 487-line module-level state machine
(4 globals: `globalCreditsCache` / `globalSubscribers` /
`globalFetchingPromise` / `creditsRefreshPending`) to a single
`useQuery` keyed by uid. RQ owns the cache, dedup, cross-instance
fanout, polling.
- 24-field facade preserved → 13 callsites unchanged.
- New `web/src/hooks/queries/billing/keys.ts` with uid-scoped
`billingKeys.credits(uid)` factory.

## Pattern checklist (PR-b2 round 3 / 4 lessons applied up-front)

- [x] `refetchOnMount: 'always'` matches PR-a / PR-b1 / PR-b2
- [x] `cancelQueries` before any `setQueryData` write (mutation path +
`credits-refresh-data` event listener)
- [x] uid-scoped queryKey (D4)
- [x] `error` derived from `query.error`, not stored
- [x] `refresh()` uses `cancelRefetch: false` so concurrent calls share
one in-flight (preserves the pre-RQ `globalFetchingPromise` N+1
protection)

## What changes

- `useState + globalSubscribers` → `useQuery` (cross-instance fanout is
RQ-native)
- `setInterval(refresh, 3min)` → `refetchInterval: 3 * 60_000`
- `auth-state-changed` listener: on uid change, `removeQueries({
queryKey: billingKeys.credits(prev) })` + clear module mirror
- `credits-refresh` listener: `invalidateQueries` (no more microtask
batching — RQ debounces internally)
- `credits-refresh-data` listener: **`cancelQueries` + `setQueryData`**
so an in-flight focus refetch can't overwrite the pushed payload
- `checkCreditsEnough` reads `queryClient.getQueryData` for cache-hit;
on miss/insufficient runs API + `cancelQueries + setQueryData`
- Mock override path unchanged (render-time `getMockBillingState()`);
query's `enabled` gate now also checks `!activeMock`, so flipping the
mock flag in localStorage takes effect immediately

## Module-level mirror for `getCachedCreditsCheck`

The non-React caller (`useSendMessage`'s pre-flight) needs sync access.
The hook keeps a module-level `cacheMirror` in sync with `query.data`
via a `useEffect`, with a 1-minute TTL gate so long-idle reads return
null. **`useSendMessage` is unchanged in this PR** — option A from the
design discussion (facade-preservation over collapsing the mirror).

## Removed exports

- `getCachedCredits()` was deprecated, returned 0, no callers.
- `clearCreditsCache()` kept as a test helper (resets the mirror); no
longer touches a real cache.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` clean
- [x] `npx vitest run tests/unit/hooks/useBillingCredits.unit.spec.ts` —
30/30 pass
- [x] **Sharp regression test** for `credits-refresh-data` race:
instruments an AbortSignal listener inside the queryFn so the assertion
verifies the actual cancel propagation (not just the final cache value,
which had a false-pass in an early draft). Verified the test fails when
`cancelQueries` is removed.
- [x] `npx vitest run` against 52 consumer component test files
(UserMenu / UserCard / SideNav / CreditsDisplay / billing /
AgentChatClient / CanvasClient / ModelDegradationBanner / onboarding /
etc.) — 1005/1005 pass; facade preservation confirmed
- [ ] Smoke: `/canvas`, `/chat`, `/claw-settings/usage`, top-bar credits
chip, paywall flows
- [ ] Smoke: cross-tab account switch (verify cache miss + new bucket on
uid change)

## Not in scope

- `useSendMessage` switch to RQ — kept on module mirror (option A);
deferred to PR-c
- `PaywallContent / InvoiceHistory / SubscriptionPanel` — spec PR-e
(high risk, runs after b/c/d are green; staging visual regression)
- 24-field facade slimming

Refs: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-react-query-migration-v2.md`
PR series: #1455 (PR-a / b1 merged) → #1523 (PR-b2 in merge queue) →
this (PR-b3)

## Pattern checklist (PR-b2 round 3 / 4 lessons applied up-front)

- [x] `refetchOnMount: 'always'` matches PR-a / PR-b1 / PR-b2
- [x] `cancelQueries` before any `setQueryData` write (mutation path +
`credits-refresh-data` event listener)
- [x] uid-scoped queryKey (D4)
- [x] `error` derived from `query.error`, not stored
- [x] `refresh()` uses `cancelRefetch: false` so concurrent calls share
one in-flight (preserves the pre-RQ `globalFetchingPromise` N+1
protection)

## What changes

- `useState + globalSubscribers` → `useQuery` (cross-instance fanout is
RQ-native)
- `setInterval(refresh, 3min)` → `refetchInterval: 3 * 60_000`
- `auth-state-changed` listener: on uid change, `removeQueries({
queryKey: billingKeys.credits(prev) })` + clear module mirror
- `credits-refresh` listener: `invalidateQueries` (no more microtask
batching — RQ debounces internally)
- `credits-refresh-data` listener: **`cancelQueries` + `setQueryData`**
so an in-flight focus refetch can't overwrite the pushed payload
- `checkCreditsEnough` reads `queryClient.getQueryData` for cache-hit;
on miss/insufficient runs API + `cancelQueries + setQueryData`
- Mock override path unchanged (render-time `getMockBillingState()`);
query's `enabled` gate now also checks `!activeMock`, so flipping the
mock flag in localStorage takes effect immediately

## Module-level mirror for `getCachedCreditsCheck`

The non-React caller (`useSendMessage`'s pre-flight) needs sync access.
The hook keeps a module-level `cacheMirror` in sync with `query.data`
via a `useEffect`, with a 1-minute TTL gate so long-idle reads return
null. **`useSendMessage` is unchanged in this PR** — option A from the
design discussion (facade-preservation over collapsing the mirror).

## Removed exports

- `getCachedCredits()` was deprecated, returned 0, no callers.
- `clearCreditsCache()` kept as a test helper (resets the mirror); no
longer touches a real cache.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` clean
- [x] `npx vitest run tests/unit/hooks/useBillingCredits.unit.spec.ts` —
30/30 pass
- [x] **Sharp regression test** for `credits-refresh-data` race:
instruments an AbortSignal listener inside the queryFn so the assertion
verifies the actual cancel propagation (not just the final cache value,
which had a false-pass in an early draft). Verified the test fails when
`cancelQueries` is removed.
- [x] `npx vitest run` against 52 consumer component test files
(UserMenu / UserCard / SideNav / CreditsDisplay / billing /
AgentChatClient / CanvasClient / ModelDegradationBanner / onboarding /
etc.) — 1005/1005 pass; facade preservation confirmed
- [ ] Smoke: `/canvas`, `/chat`, `/claw-settings/usage`, top-bar credits
chip, paywall flows
- [ ] Smoke: cross-tab account switch (verify cache miss + new bucket on
uid change)

## Not in scope

- `useSendMessage` switch to RQ — kept on module mirror (option A);
deferred to PR-c
- `PaywallContent / InvoiceHistory / SubscriptionPanel` — spec PR-e
(high risk, runs after b/c/d are green; staging visual regression)
- 24-field facade slimming

Refs: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-react-query-migration-v2.md`
PR series: #1455 (PR-a / b1 merged) → #1523 (PR-b2 in merge queue) →
this (PR-b3)

feat(claw-interface): wire vulture dead-function detection (informational) — #1503 PR1 (#1613)

## Summary

PR1 of two for issue #1503 — wire `vulture` as a dead-function detection
CI gate for `services/claw-interface/`, in **informational mode**. PR2
(separate, ≥1 week after merge) flips the gate to hard mode.

Closes part of #1503 (acceptance criteria 1 + 2 — tool installed with
baseline + informational CI). Criteria 3 (hard gate) and 4 (memory entry
/ docs) are partially addressed here:
- Memory + project docs landed in this PR (`fe8adc66` — AGENTS.md
update; personal memory entry shipped separately as user storage).
- Hard gate flip stays for PR2.

## What lands

- `pyproject.toml` — `[tool.vulture]` config: `paths = ["app",
"scripts/ci-lint/vulture_whitelist.py"]`, `min_confidence = 80`,
`ignore_decorators` tuned against actual codebase grep (FastAPI
`@router.{get,post,put,delete,patch,head,options,websocket}` route
handlers, Pydantic v2 `@field_validator` / `@model_validator`,
`@asynccontextmanager`). Unused `@app.*` and `@computed_field` patterns
were pruned during review — codebase uses imperative
`app.add_middleware()` / `app.add_exception_handler()` wiring, not
decorator forms.
- `requirements-dev.txt` — `vulture==2.14` (exact pin; minor releases
have broken vulture's reporter format historically, mirrors the
rationale for other dev-tool pins).
- `[tool.deptry.per_rule_ignores]` — `vulture` added to the existing
`DEP002` allowlist alongside `deptry` / `import-linter` / `pre-commit` /
`ruff` (CLI-only tools, never imported).
- `scripts/ci-lint/07-dead-code.sh` — informational-mode gate (`|| true`
tolerates findings, exit ≥2 hard-fails). Tool discovery (PATH →
`.venv/bin/vulture` → `/home/node/.venvs/claw-interface/bin/vulture`)
mirrors `04-deptry.sh` line-for-line; inline comment block shows the
exact diff PR2 will apply to flip to hard mode.
- `scripts/ci-lint/vulture_whitelist.py` — docstring-only placeholder.
**Baseline is empty by design**: ran `vulture --make-whitelist app/`
inside the devcontainer at `min_confidence=80` and got zero findings.
The trigger case from #1503's body (`_deep_merge` / `_update_config` in
`openclaw_settings/helpers.py`) was already cleaned by #1505 before this
gate landed. The file ships as a docstring-only Python module; vulture
parses it cleanly and contributes zero "used-names". Maintenance rules
(shrink-only, justification-comment required, orphan-rot warning) live
in the docstring.
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` — `vulture` hook entry, matching the
`deptry` / `pyright` / `complexity` sibling style. Auto-discovered by
the python lint reusable workflow via `custom_lint_scripts_dir:
'scripts/ci-lint'`.
- `services/claw-interface/CLAUDE.md` (→ `AGENTS.md` via symlink) — one
sentence appended to the Code Quality paragraph, covering scope
decision, shrink-only rule, orphan-rot warning, and the "extend
ignore_decorators instead of per-symbol whitelist" guidance.
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-backend-vulture-dead-code.md` — the
implementation plan that drove this PR.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- **Hard gate flip** — PR2, after ≥1 week observation window. Exact diff
is documented in `07-dead-code.sh`'s trailing comment block.
- **Baseline whitelist entries** — currently empty by design. New
entries get added as FPs surface; the docstring documents how to triage
and justify them.

## Test plan

- [x] `vulture --make-whitelist app/` inside devcontainer at rollout
config: zero findings (verified — empty baseline)
- [x] Bare `vulture` (which reads `[tool.vulture].paths` from
`pyproject.toml`) exits 0 cleanly in devcontainer
- [x] Per-task spec compliance + code quality review for each of Tasks 1
/ 3 / 6 (subagent-driven; review iterations absorbed)
- [x] Final cross-task review: no doc-code drift, no cross-file
inconsistency, runtime prediction matches design intent
- [ ] CI: `code-quality / lint-and-test` passes — includes
`04-deptry.sh` (needed the new DEP002 allowlist entry), pyright, ruff,
file-length, complexity, importlinter, and the new `07-dead-code.sh`
itself
- [ ] CI: `python-code-quality / build-and-test` passes — auto-discovers
`07-dead-code.sh` via `custom_lint_scripts_dir`

## Notes for reviewers

- Eight implementation commits + the plan doc, sequenced to tell the
story: deps + config → review fix (pruned unused decorators) → script +
hook → review fix (venv discovery) → review fix (style alignment) → docs
→ baseline-empty doc → deptry allowlist. Each commit references #1503 in
the message body.
- The worktree devcontainer has a pre-existing quirk where `git
rev-parse --show-toplevel` fails inside the container (the worktree's
`.git` is a file pointing to a host path not mounted in the container).
This affects **all** sibling ci-lint scripts (`04-deptry.sh`,
`02-import-linter.sh`, `06-importlinter-repo-sync.sh`) equally — not
introduced by this PR. Run pre-commit from the main checkout (not a
worktree) until that gets fixed upstream.

Refs #1503.

commit.message:
ci: delegate pr-size-check to srp-actions reusable (#1615)

## Summary

Replaces the local `.github/workflows/pr-size-check-reusable.yml` (148
lines) with `uses:` calls to
[srp-actions/.github/workflows/pr-size-check.yml@main](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/blob/main/.github/workflows/pr-size-check.yml)
(merged as srp-actions PR #63). The new reusable is fully self-contained
— the diff-counting bash logic + EXCLUDES list are inlined into its
heredoc, so consumer repos need no scripts at all to use it.

This finishes the 3-PR web-CI-extraction series (web-code-quality,
codex-review, pr-size-check).

## Three caller workflows updated

| File | Job | post_comment |
|---|---|---|
| `code-quality.yml` | `size` (fail-fast gate before heavy jobs) |
`false` |
| `pr-size-check.yml` | `size` (code-event entry, sticky comment) |
`true` |
| `pr-size-label-refresh.yml` | `size` (`size-override` toggle refresh)
| `true` |

## Files changed

- **Deleted**: `.github/workflows/pr-size-check-reusable.yml` (148
lines)
- **Modified caller workflows**: `code-quality.yml`,
`pr-size-check.yml`, `pr-size-label-refresh.yml` (one-line `uses:` swap
each + comment updates)
- **Kept**: `scripts/check-pr-size.sh` — still used by `.husky/pre-push`
for fast pre-push feedback
- **Header comments updated**: `scripts/check-pr-size.sh` and
`.husky/pre-push` now point at the srp-actions reusable as the CI source
of truth, with a note to keep the local script's EXCLUDES list in sync

## Improvements landed via srp-actions PR #63 review

The new reusable picked up several hardening fixes during its review on
srp-actions side that were NOT in the original local version:

1. **ANSI-C `\n\n` quoting fix** — over-size sticky comment's blank line
was rendering as literal `\n\n` before "体量过大..." (third quoted fragment
was plain `'...'` instead of `$'...'`)
2. **Filename-with-spaces in top-files table** — first awk used default
whitespace splitting on tab-delimited input, so `foo bar.txt` rendered
as `foo`. Fixed with `awk -F'\t'`
3. **SIGPIPE race on multi-page comment lookup** — `gh api --paginate
... | head -n1` under `set -o pipefail` could abort if gh streams more
than head reads. Fixed with `mapfile -t < <(...)` to read full output

## Verify after merge

- [ ] Open any PR — confirm the sticky PR-size comment posts (now with
the bilingual EN+中文 surface from srp-actions, but content body still
Chinese per existing convention)
- [ ] Create an oversize test PR (~3000+ lines) — confirm:
  - sticky comment shows correct status ❌ and threshold
  - blank line renders before "体量过大..." (not literal `\n\n`)
  - `size / size-check` status check goes red
- [ ] Add `size-override` label to the oversize test PR — confirm:
  - `pr-size-label-refresh` re-runs
  - status check transitions to ✅ and comment updates
- [ ] Verify husky pre-push still runs the local script (push a normal
commit; check pre-push output)

## Companion / context

- srp-actions PR #61 (web-code-quality-v1) — ✅ merged
- srp-actions PR #62 (codex-review) — ✅ merged
- srp-actions PR #63 (pr-size-check) — ✅ merged ← this PR consumes it
- srp-actions PR #64/65/66 — ✅ merged (claude/codex self-callers +
hotfix)
- ecap PR #1611 (web-quality caller) — ✅ merged
- ecap PR #1612 (codex-review caller) — ✅ merged
- ecap PR #_this_ (pr-size-check caller) — finishes the migration

commit.message:
fix(arch-review): line-anchor RESOLVED_END marker in manual_suffix extract (#1614)

## Summary

The `manual_suffix` extraction in `claude-arch-review.yaml` used a plain
substring `split` on `<!-- ARCH_REVIEW:RESOLVED_END -->`. The intro
block legitimately references that same marker as a backtick-quoted
literal ("place free text AFTER the `<!-- ARCH_REVIEW:RESOLVED_END -->`
marker"), so `split` matched the intro reference and treated everything
past it (the real INTRO_END + SUMMARY/FINDINGS/RESOLVED blocks) as
manual notes — which then got re-emitted as a "suffix" below the next
run's managed region.

Repeated weekly, this embedded a full stale ARCH_REVIEW block copy into
the manual-notes region each run.

**Observed in production** (before this fix):

| Issue | RESOLVED_END markers | Lines |
|---|---:|---:|
| #365 (ios) | 4 | 187 |
| #366 (claw-interface) | 4 | 337 |
| #368 (web) | 5 | 432 |

**Fix**: switch to a line-anchored regex (`^<!--
ARCH_REVIEW:RESOLVED_END -->$` with `re.MULTILINE`) so the match fires
only on standalone marker lines, ignoring the intro's backtick
reference. Anchor on the LAST occurrence so that any body still in a
corrupted state self-heals on the next run.

**Data cleanup (already applied, ahead of merge)**: the three issue
bodies have been rewritten via `gh issue edit` to keep only the first
managed block + legitimate manual notes (only #368 had any — the
"Progress on current baseline" table + Top-5 priorities + "Resolved
since the original" block) + canonical footer.

| Issue | Lines (pre) | Lines (post) |
|---|---:|---:|
| #365 (ios) | 187 | 68 |
| #366 (claw-interface) | 337 | 121 |
| #368 (web) | 432 | 160 |

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`python3 -c "import yaml;
yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-arch-review.yaml'))"`)
- [x] Data cleanup verified post-edit: each issue has exactly 1
line-anchored RESOLVED_END marker
- [ ] Manual `workflow_dispatch` after merge on one module to confirm
next run does not re-introduce corruption
- `gh workflow run claude-arch-review.yaml -f modules=arch-review:web -f
language=zh`
- [ ] Confirm next Monday 09:00 UTC scheduled run produces bodies with
stable line-count (no embedded block growth)

**Fix**: switch to a line-anchored regex (`^<!--
ARCH_REVIEW:RESOLVED_END -->$` with `re.MULTILINE`) so the match fires
only on standalone marker lines, ignoring the intro's backtick
reference. Anchor on the LAST occurrence so that any body still in a
corrupted state self-heals on the next run.

**Data cleanup (already applied, ahead of merge)**: the three issue
bodies have been rewritten via `gh issue edit` to keep only the first
managed block + legitimate manual notes (only #368 had any — the
"Progress on current baseline" table + Top-5 priorities + "Resolved
since the original" block) + canonical footer.

| Issue | Lines (pre) | Lines (post) |
|---|---:|---:|
| #365 (ios) | 187 | 68 |
| #366 (claw-interface) | 337 | 121 |
| #368 (web) | 432 | 160 |

yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-arch-review.yaml'))"`)

ci(web): delegate web-quality to srp-actions/web-code-quality-v1 (#1611)

## Summary

Replaces the inline 185-line `web-quality` job in
`.github/workflows/code-quality.yml` with a ~20-line `uses:` call to the
new reusable workflow in srp-actions ([srp-actions PR
#61](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/pull/61), already
merged).

Behavior is equivalent: same lint / lint:ci / tsc / tests / coverage /
jscpd / coverage badge / Feishu notify surface — now with **3-job
internal parallelism** (lint-and-typecheck / test / summary) instead of
one long sequential job. Wall-clock should improve from ~250s sequential
to ~max(120s, 130s) + 5s summary ≈ 135s for the typical web suite.

## Inputs wired in this caller

```yaml
uses: SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/.github/workflows/web-code-quality-v1.yml@main
secrets: inherit
with:
  repo_name: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}
  runner_name: ubuntu-latest-m
  enable_coverage_badge: true
  coverage_gist_id: '7780840fe6a8c160e1a73fdbcd5b786a'
  coverage_badge_filename: 'web-coverage-badge.json'
  pre_lint_scripts: |
    web/scripts/check-ignores-shrink-only.sh
    web/scripts/check-no-raw-fetch-shrink-only.sh
    web/scripts/check-svg-ignores-shrink-only.sh
    web/scripts/check-filename-shrink-only.sh
  jscpd_configs: |
    web/.jscpd.src.json
    web/.jscpd.tests.json
```

Caller-side artifacts kept unchanged (reusable doesn't know about them):
- `web/scripts/check-*-shrink-only.sh` — 4 baseline checks, passed via
`pre_lint_scripts`
-
`web/scripts/ci-lint/{00-run-all,01-import-boundaries,02-dead-code}.sh`
— orchestrated by `pnpm lint:ci` which is what the reusable invokes
- `web/.jscpd.src.json` + `web/.jscpd.tests.json` — passed via
`jscpd_configs`

## Status check names changing

Before: `code-quality / web-quality` (one check).
After: `code-quality / web-quality / lint-and-typecheck` + `code-quality
/ web-quality / test` + `code-quality / web-quality / summary`.

The aggregator `code-quality` job at the bottom of `code-quality.yml`
continues to gate via `needs: web-quality` — GitHub Actions
transparently waits for **all** reusable subjobs, so the aggregator
status is unaffected. Branch protection should already require
`code-quality` (the aggregator), not the inner `web-quality` per-job —
please verify the ruleset before merging.

## Test plan

- [ ] PR's own CI run (this PR is a `web/**`-touching PR? It only
touches `.github/workflows/`, so `web-quality` job should skip via
`paths:`) — actually no, this PR doesn't touch web/. So **the new
reusable won't actually exercise on this PR**. The first real exercise
will be on the next normal web/ PR after this lands.
- [ ] Suggested: open a follow-up trivial web/ PR after this merges to
verify the 3-job structure shows up correctly + coverage / jscpd numbers
match recent main runs
- [ ] Compare coverage badge update on next push to main (badge should
still update)
- [ ] On purpose break ESLint in a test PR → confirm
`lint-and-typecheck` job goes red but `test` job still runs (the whole
point of the parallel split)

## Companion PRs

- srp-actions PR #61: web-code-quality-v1 reusable definition (✅ merged)
- srp-actions PR #64: claude-review pure reusable + self-caller (open) —
unrelated to this PR but part of the same reusable-extraction series

commit.message:
ci: delegate codex-review to srp-actions/codex-review (#1612)

## Summary

Replaces the inline 281-line `.github/workflows/codex-review.yaml` with
a 33-line `uses:` call to the new Codex Auto Review reusable in
srp-actions ([PR
#62](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/pull/62), already
merged on that side).

Behavior is equivalent: same pre-fetch diff → minimize stale reviews →
build prompt → run `openai/codex-action@v1` → parse verdict → post
comment → set status + label flow. Centralized so other repos
(zooclaw-extras etc.) can adopt with one `uses:` block.

## Caller config

```yaml
uses: SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/.github/workflows/codex-review.yaml@main
secrets:
  AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY }}
with:
  model: gpt-5.4
  responses_api_endpoint: ${{ vars.AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
  allowed_bots: srp-claude-assistant
  claude_md_paths: |
    CLAUDE.md
    web/CLAUDE.md
    services/claw-interface/CLAUDE.md
```

## Latent bug fix bundled

The inline workflow used `allow-bot-users: srp-claude-assistant` as the
codex-action input. **That's not a valid input** for
`openai/codex-action@v1` — actionlint catches it (the action's actual
inputs are `allow-bots` and `allow-users`). The flag was silently
swallowed, meaning the bot-exemption hasn't been working. The reusable
uses the correct `allow-bots`, so the exemption will actually take
effect after this lands.

## Test plan

- [ ] After merge, open any PR — confirm the Codex review comment still
posts with the `## 🤖 Codex Review` prefix
- [ ] Confirm verdict parsing still works (REQUEST_CHANGES turns job
red; NEED_HUMAN_REVIEW adds label; APPROVE keeps job green)
- [ ] Confirm stale reviews from prior pushes get minimized (OUTDATED)
on re-push
- [ ] Confirm dependabot PRs skip codex-review cleanly (job marked
skipped)
- [ ] Confirm an `srp-claude-assistant` PR (e.g.
dependabot-lockfile-refresh re-push) now passes the bot-exempt check —
previously it didn't because `allow-bot-users` was a no-op

commit.message:
feat(arch-review): parametrize output language, default to Simplified Chinese (#1608)

## Summary

- Add `language` `workflow_dispatch` input (`zh`|`en`, default `zh`) and
a `Resolve output language` step with shell-level `${VAR:-zh}` fallback
for schedule-triggered runs (where `github.event.inputs.language` is
null, so the input default alone wouldn't kick in).
- Inject a language directive at the top of the Claude prompt: finding
**titles**, **descriptions**, **recommendations**, and the **Summary**
are written in the configured language; HTML markers, F-IDs, field
labels (`**Severity**`, `**First seen**`, …), severity/category enum
values, and markdown section headers stay in English so the downstream
Python compose step parses unchanged.
- **Baseline migration**: prior still-present findings are preserved
**verbatim** — no LLM translation — so the F-ID monitoring stream stays
stable across language switches. Existing English findings in the three
open `arch-review` issues will gradually be replaced by Chinese ones as
they get resolved + replaced by new findings.

Next Monday's scheduled run (09:00 UTC) will produce Chinese findings by
default. No issue-body migration required (workflow rebuilds from its
own prior body each week).

## Test plan

- [ ] `python3 -c "import yaml;
yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-arch-review.yaml'))"`
parses
- [ ] Manual dispatch with `language=zh` on one module — verify new
findings are Chinese, HTML markers / F-IDs / `**Severity**: High` field
stay ASCII, Python compose step has no `::error::`
- `gh workflow run claude-arch-review.yaml -f modules=arch-review:web -f
language=zh`
- [ ] Manual dispatch with `language=en` regression check
- `gh workflow run claude-arch-review.yaml -f
modules=arch-review:claw-interface -f language=en`
- [ ] Schedule fallback simulation: `INPUT_LANGUAGE="" bash -c 'echo
"${INPUT_LANGUAGE:-zh}"'` prints `zh`
- [ ] Next Monday 09:00 UTC schedule run produces Chinese findings
across all three modules

commit.message:
fix: 清 31 条 Dependabot + 3 条 CodeQL 安全告警(收编 #1576) (#1607)

## Summary

合并 dependabot PR #1576 的 8 个 minor/patch bump,叠加 GitHub Security 上 31 条
open Dependabot alerts + 3 条 CodeQL alerts 的全量修复。**Supersedes
#1576**(merge 后关 #1576)。

PR #1576 处于 \`CONFLICTING\` 状态:Dependabot 把 \`@opennextjs/cloudflare\` 从
1.19.4 bump 到 1.19.5,但没同步 bump \`wrangler\`(1.19.5 peer 要求
\`wrangler@^4.86.0\`,主仓在它开 PR 之后又把 wrangler 升到 \`^4.85.0\`,两边都不够新)— 单文件
\`web/package.json\` 一行冲突。本 PR 把 wrangler 一起带到 \`^4.86.0\` 解开冲突,顺便把整批 31
条 Dependabot 安全告警与 3 条 CodeQL 告警一并清零。

## 直接依赖 (web/package.json)

| 包 | 旧 | 新 | 来源 |
|----|----|----|------|
| next | ^15.5.15 | ^15.5.18 | security (13 alerts: middleware bypass /
SSRF / DoS / XSS) |
| mermaid | ^11.14.0 | ^11.15.0 | security (4 alerts) |
| eslint-config-next | ^15.5.15 | ^15.5.18 | 与 next 主版本对齐 |
| wrangler | ^4.85.0 | ^4.86.0 | peer dep (@opennextjs/cloudflare
1.19.5+) |
| @opennextjs/cloudflare | ^1.19.4 | ^1.19.5 | #1576 |
| dompurify | ^3.4.1 | ^3.4.2 | #1576 |
| marked | ^18.0.0 | ^18.0.3 | #1576 |
| globals | ^17.5.0 | ^17.6.0 | #1576 |
| knip | 6.7.0 | 6.10.0 | #1576 |
| postcss | ^8.5.10 | ^8.5.13 | #1576 |
| prettier-plugin-tailwindcss | ^0.7.3 | ^0.8.0 | #1576 |
| zod | ^4.3.5 | ^4.4.1 | #1576 |

## 间接依赖 (pnpm.overrides,range-scoped)

| 包 | override | 覆盖的 alerts |
|----|----------|---------------|
| protobufjs | ^7.5.5 → ^7.5.6 | 7 (DoS / code injection / prototype
pollution) |
| @protobufjs/utf8@<1.1.1 | ^1.1.1 | 1 (overlong UTF-8) |
| fast-uri@<3.1.2 | ^3.1.2 | 2 (host confusion + path traversal) |
| fast-xml-builder@<1.1.7 | ^1.1.7 | 2 (attr 绕过 + 注释 regex 绕过) |
| ip-address@<10.1.1 | ^10.1.1 | 1 (Address6 HTML XSS) |

range-scoped 写法避免 blanket caret 把跨 major consumer 反向 downgrade。

## CodeQL Python 修复

| 文件:行 | rule | 修复 |
|---------|------|------|
| services/claw-interface/app/services/openclaw/agent_deploy.py:186 |
py/clear-text-logging-sensitive-data | list comp 内的 agent_id 走
\`safe_enum\` 断 taint flow |
|
services/claw-interface/app/services/openclaw_client/_channels.py:22,26
| py/clear-text-logging-sensitive-data | \`write_bot_file\` 同函数两处 path
log 走 \`safe_enum\` |
|
services/claw-interface/app/routes/openclaw_settings/weixin_helpers.py:52
| py/polynomial-redos | 删 2 条 regex,用 \`str.strip("-")\` + 早裁 \`[:128]\`
|

\`str.strip\` 是 C 实现的线性扫描,无 NFA 回溯,既断 CodeQL 的 polynomial-redos
推断又消除潜在路径;输入端早裁双重防御。

## 操作要点

- **pnpm caret quirk**:lockfile 已有 \`protobufjs@7.5.5\` 时写 \`^7.5.6\`
不会触发 re-resolve;用 exact-pin → install → 改回 caret 三步绕开。
- **minimumReleaseAge**:本地 \`pnpm install --config.minimumReleaseAge=0\`
旁路 7 天门槛;CI \`--frozen-lockfile\` 不做 age check 直装。
- **wrangler 反向 forward**:#1576 单独 bump \`@opennextjs/cloudflare\` 没带
wrangler,导致 peer 不齐;本 PR 把 wrangler bump 到 \`^4.86.0\` 满足 1.19.5 peer
dep。

## 保留未动

- \`postcss\` 还有 \`8.4.31\` transitive in \`next@15.5.18\` bundle — 不强
override 避免破坏 next 内部 CSS pipeline;CVE 上下文是 untrusted CSS stringify,next

feat(claw-interface): introduce Profile schema and dedicated profile_repo for gem_account (#1588)

## Summary

Introduces typed Pydantic models for the CSFLE-encrypted `gem_account`
collection and consolidates all profile-collection MongoDB operations
into a dedicated `app.database.profile_repo` — mirroring the `Account` /
`user_repo` split landed in #1574 and #1585.

### What changed

**New `app/schema/profile.py`** — `Profile` (read) and `ProfileUpdate`
(write) models matching the JSON schema of the `gem_account` collection.
Read uses `extra="allow"` to tolerate Mongo `_id` and future fields;
write uses `extra="forbid"` to catch typos before they hit the encrypted
collection. CSFLE-sensitive text fields (`avatar`, `bio`,
`phone_number`) accept `None` so `sanitize_profile`-masked reads
round-trip cleanly. `uid: int` matches the `Int64` storage and
`profile_repo.get_by_uid(uid_int: int)` signature.

**`app/database/profile_repo.py`** gains three operations:
- `get_by_uid(uid_int) -> Profile | None` — single-doc fetch (was
already there as `dict | None`, now typed).
- `batch_get_by_uids(list[int]) -> list[Profile]` — relocated from
`user_repo.batch_get_profiles`, retyped.
- `find_uids_by_filter(query, limit=1000) -> list[int]` — relocated from
`user_repo.search_profiles`, retyped to return just the projected uids
(the only field crossing the wire and the only thing the caller
consumes).

**`app/database/user_repo.py`** drops its `# ── Profile (gem_account)
──` section and `PROFILE_COLLECTION` import — completing the cohesion
split. The C1 import-linter contract (only `app.database` may import
`mongo`) and C4 (repo independence) both remain green.

**Callers updated** with a unified shim: `Profile.to_response_dict()`
(added on the schema) does `model_dump(exclude_unset=True) + strip _id`
in one place, preserving the prior "only fields actually in Mongo
round-trip out" payload shape for the 4 downstream callers
(`routes/user.py`, `routes/invite_code.py`, `services/profile.py`,
`services/user/enrichment.py`). The `exclude_unset` rationale lives once
on the method docstring instead of being copy-pasted at each call site.

**Test fixtures consolidated**: new
`tests/unit/_builders.make_profile(uid=..., **overrides)` parallels the
existing `make_account_user`. Three test files (`test_invite_codes`,
`test_user_billing`, `test_user_enrichment_service`) shed inline 6-field
`Profile.model_validate({...})` literals.

### Commits

1. `feat(claw-interface): add Profile pydantic models for gem_account` —
schema + tests
2. `refactor(claw-interface): retype profile_repo.get_by_uid to Profile`
— single-doc migration + `get_user_profile` shim
3. `refactor(claw-interface): move profile reads into profile_repo with
typed contracts` — `batch_get_by_uids` + `find_uids_by_filter` migration
+ 4 callers + 5 test files
4. `refactor: simplify Profile callers via to_response_dict +
make_profile builder` — extracts shared shim, drops redundant
reverse-map rebuild, consolidates test fixtures

## Test plan

Verified inside `service-profile-repo-bill` devcontainer:

- [x] `pytest tests/unit -q --cov=app` — **3083 passed**, 2 deselected
(environment-only deptry tests that need host `.git` access; pass in
CI), 4 warnings, 9:52 wall. Touched-module coverage:
`app/database/profile_repo.py` 100%, `app/schema/profile.py` 100%,
`app/services/profile.py` 97%, `app/services/user/enrichment.py` 95%,
`app/routes/invite_code.py` 96%.
- [x] `npx pyright <touched files>` — 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0
informations.
- [x] `ruff check <touched files>` — All checks passed.
- [x] `lint-imports` — **8 contracts kept, 0 broken** (C1
mongo-isolation and C4 repo-independence both still hold after the
move).
- [ ] BDD `tests/bdd/features/user_list.feature` requires
`TEST_MONGODB_HOST=127.0.0.1 MONGODB_USER= MONGODB_PASSWORD=` overrides
per `services/claw-interface/CLAUDE.md`; not run locally. Step-defs in
`tests/bdd/step_defs/test_user_list.py` were updated to patch
`profile_repo` alongside `user_repo` to match the new module boundary —
worth a manual BDD run if any reviewer is concerned.

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- The `.model_dump()` shims in `Profile.to_response_dict()` callers
parallel the pending `Account` cleanup tracked elsewhere — eventually
`routes/invite_code` and `services/user/enrichment` could consume typed
`Profile` end-to-end, but that requires touching `sanitize_profile` /
`check_and_add_admin_permission`, which is broader than this PR's scope.
- `services/profile.get_user_profile` still returns `dict | None` for
backwards compatibility with its 4 callers; making it return `Profile |
None` would need updates to those callers (`routes/user.py` × 3,
`services/user/enrichment.py`, `auth/dependencies.py`).

commit.message:
feat: harden Antom Alipay payment integration (#1592)

Supersedes #749.

## Summary
- Integrate Antom/Alipay as a provider-aware payment path alongside
Stripe.
- Harden Antom Cashier one-time payment creation with DB-authoritative
amount/currency, redirect URL allowlisting, and signed API response
verification.
- Add official Subscription Payment create/cancel plumbing for when
Antom enables the product on the merchant account.
- Make webhook/recovery fulfillment idempotent across
success/failure/cancel paths.
- Add refund compensation that refunds through Antom and voids
previously granted Billing Gateway credits with deterministic revoke
transaction IDs.
- Treat refunded/compensating order states as non-success on the
frontend payment success page.
- Document the rollout plan and current Subscription Payment activation
gate.

## Sandbox Notes
- Cashier top-up path was tested end-to-end in sandbox through Antom
redirect, inquiry/webhook recovery, order paid, and credits grant.
- Compensation was tested against sandbox refund plus Billing Gateway
credit void replay behavior.
- Subscription Payment E2E remains blocked until Antom enables that
product for the merchant account.

## Verification
- Python pre-commit hooks: passed
- Targeted backend pytest: `68 passed`
- Targeted pyright: `0 errors`
- Frontend unit tests/typecheck were run before this commit and passed

commit.message:
refactor(web): prefill composer instead of auto-sending landing query (#1602)

## Summary

The landing-context flow (`?sp=<specialist_id>` +
`localStorage['ecap:landingContext']`) used to auto-send the initial
query into chat once the agent switch settled. This refactor replaces
the auto-send with a **prefill into the composer**, so the user reviews
the prompt and submits manually.

### Why

The user wanted the landing-page initial query to land in the chat input
rather than fire automatically — gives them a chance to edit before
sending and avoids surprise sends on the very first interaction.

### What changed

- **`useLandingContextFlow.ts`** — `onSwitchAgent(agentId)` →
`onSwitchAgent(agentId, prefillText?)`. Dropped `onSendMessage` and
`currentAgentId` from the hook's interface. Phase 5 (`delivering`)
collapses to `cleanup`: mark delivered + clear localStorage. Removed the
closure-staleness gate that waited for `currentAgentId ===
resolvedAgentId` — no longer needed since prefill is local state, not a
session-routed message.
- **`GenClawClient.tsx`** — `handleLandingSwitchAgent` builds a single
`router.replace('/chat?agent_id=X&q=Y')` (or `/chat?q=Y` on Main Agent
fallback), folding the agent switch and prefill into one atomic
navigation.
- **`GenClawInput.tsx`** — new effect to react to `initialInput`
becoming non-empty post-mount when the composer is empty and no
persisted draft exists. Without this, the mount-only lazy initializer
misses URL updates from in-page navigation (the existing `?q=` plumbing
only worked at mount, which is fine for `useDeepLinkHireFlow`'s
navigate-away pattern but not for our in-`/chat` agent switch).

### What's preserved

- ECA-641 §4.2 fallbacks (specialist not in catalog / hire failure →
Main Agent + prefill).
- Idempotency (`markLandingDelivered` + `clearLandingContext` still fire
so a refresh doesn't re-prefill from stale storage).
- 24h TTL on stored context, cross-validation of URL `sp` ↔ storage
`specialist_id`.
- Catalog-failure 8s timeout escape hatch.
- Persisted-draft and in-progress-typing protection: the composer's new
effect is narrowly gated (`input === ''` + no sessionStorage draft) so
it never clobbers user state.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run
tests/unit/hooks/useLandingContextFlow.unit.spec.ts
tests/unit/app/chat/GenClawInput.unit.spec.tsx` — 58 passing
- [x] `pnpm vitest run tests/unit/lib/landing-context.unit.spec.ts` — 29
passing (no changes to lib, sanity check)
- [x] Full `pnpm vitest run` — 4523 tests passing (1 pre-existing todo,
unrelated)
- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` — clean
- [ ] Manual: load `/chat?sp=<id>` with a matching `ecap:landingContext`
in `localStorage` (non-empty `initial_query.text`); verify chat opens on
the specialist, the composer is prefilled with the text, no auto-send
fires, send button works.
- [ ] Manual: load with `initial_query.text === ''` — verify switch
happens, composer stays empty, delivered marker set.
- [ ] Manual: load with a specialist NOT in the catalog — verify Main
Agent fallback with prefill preserved.
- [ ] Manual: refresh after delivery — verify no re-prefill (idempotency
holds).

commit.message:
fix(web): prevent login modal reopen race (#1601)

## Summary

Fixes a race where rapidly reopening the login modal (close → open
within 300ms) caused a stale exit timer to unmount the freshly-opened
modal.

- Track the in-flight transition timer in a `useRef` so each `isOpen`
flip cancels the prior handle before scheduling the next.
- Use a single `setTimeout`-based primitive for both the enter (0ms,
kicks the CSS transition) and exit (300ms, gates `shouldRender`).
`requestAnimationFrame` + `setTimeout` were initially used together, but
their id-spaces aren't unified across all environments (rAF polyfill /
throttled-tab fallback) — cancelling an rAF id with
`cancelAnimationFrame` can silently no-op while the callback still
fires.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm lint` — passes
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` — passes
- [x] `pnpm test:unit` — 4522 passed, 1 todo. New regression test `does
not let a stale close timer unmount the modal after it reopens` covers
the race; pre-existing tests still pass.
- [ ] Manual: open the login modal, close it, immediately reopen within
~300ms — modal stays mounted instead of flickering away.
